mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held below its trailing 12-month average signals an accommodative monetary stance that lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of other macro conditions.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.98% remains near multi-year lows as of 2013-11
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns during low-rate periods are not significantly higher than during high-rate periods (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the unemployment rate that crosses below the prior 6-month rolling minimum signals accelerating labor market tightening, prompting a short-horizon risk-on repricing that produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as investors extrapolate improving consumer income prospects.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below its trailing 6-month rolling minimum
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.5% in 2013-11 continuing multi-year downtrend
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following a new 6-month UNRATE low are not significantly different from unconditional 5-day returns (t-test p > 0.10), or hit rate below 50% over a 3-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales growth accelerates while the fed funds rate remains low, the joint condition of strong consumer demand and cheap financing amplifies corporate earnings expectations more than either signal alone, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days; if the fed funds rate is elevated, the same retail sales acceleration is offset by tightening financial conditions and the effect disappears.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS 385448 million with FEDFUNDS at 1.98% as of late 2013
failure_condition: Interaction term coefficient (RSAFS growth x low-rate indicator) is not significant at 10% level over rolling 3-year windows, or 60-day forward returns under the joint trigger are statistically indistinguishable from RSAFS-only trigger returns.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth positively predicts S&P 500 forward returns only during expansionary regimes (INDPRO year-over-year growth > 0); during contractionary regimes the same growth signal loses predictive power or reverses because rising output amid weak demand signals inventory overbuild rather than genuine expansion.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 111.18 in 2013-10 consistent with ongoing expansion
failure_condition: 20-day forward return correlation with INDPRO growth is not meaningfully higher (by at least 0.05 Rank IC) in expansion regime vs contraction regime across a full business cycle sample.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts act as a leading indicator of broader economic momentum; a sustained increase in HOUST over the trailing 3 months signals improving construction-linked demand and consumer confidence, translating into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as the signal is gradually priced into cyclical equities.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1032 thousand units in 2013-10 above post-crisis lows
failure_condition: Rank IC between 3-month HOUST change and forward 20d SPX returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or sign of correlation flips persistently across subsamples.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI year-over-year inflation crosses above 3%, markets price in higher probability of imminent Fed tightening, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days until inflation recedes below the threshold.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 215.132 in 2013-11 implying YoY inflation well below 3%
failure_condition: Forward 20d returns during CPI>3% YoY periods are not significantly more negative than baseline (p > 0.10), or hit rate of negative returns under trigger is below 50% over a full sample.

mechanism_narrative: A month with simultaneous strong payroll growth (PAYEMS month-over-month increase above trailing 12-month average) and robust retail sales growth (RSAFS month-over-month increase above trailing 12-month average) signals a self-reinforcing income-spending loop that produces a short-lived positive S&P 500 reaction over the next 5 trading days beyond what either series predicts alone.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS MoM change > trailing 12-month average AND RSAFS MoM change > trailing 12-month average
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 137754 thousand and RSAFS at 385448 million as of 2013-11
failure_condition: Joint-trigger 5-day forward returns are not significantly higher than the average of single-trigger (PAYEMS-only or RSAFS-only) returns, tested via interaction coefficient significance below 10% over rolling samples.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of below-trend real GDP growth (GDPC1 YoY growth < 2%), positive surprises in industrial production carry outsized signaling value for an impending cyclical recovery, producing stronger positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days than the same INDPRO signal would generate during a robust-growth regime, where the marginal information content of INDPRO is diluted.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11701.9 billion chained dollars as of mid-2013 with sub-2% trend growth
failure_condition: 60-day forward return sensitivity to INDPRO changes (regression slope) is not meaningfully larger in low-GDP-growth regime than in high-GDP-growth regime across a rolling 5-year comparison, or difference is not statistically significant at 10% level.
